symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive worsening exertional dyspnoea over three weeks with resting hypoxaemia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Worsening breathlessness above baseline with hypoxaemia reflects decompensation of the underlying lung and right-heart disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: New massive bilateral pitting peripheral oedema to the thighs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical dependent oedema reflects systemic venous congestion from right ventricular failure.
Bilateral symmetrical oedema with systemic congestion is not a unilateral DVT pattern.

symptoms.symptom_name: New ascites/abdominal distension with tender congestive hepatomegaly and RUQ ache

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Congestive hepatomegaly and ascites are features of advanced systemic venous congestion in right-heart failure.
When accompanied by raised JVP and RV signs, the hepatic congestion is cardiac rather than primary liver disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known severe 10-year COPD with under-used home oxygen

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe chronic hypoxaemic lung disease is the underlying cause of hypoxic pulmonary vasoconstriction and right-heart strain; oxygen under-use worsens it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, no purulent sputum, no pleuritic chest pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever and purulent sputum argues against an infective exacerbation as the primary driver.
Absence of pleuritic pain lowers but does not exclude PE as a contributor to decompensation.

symptoms.symptom_name: RV heave, loud P2, right-sided S3, and elevated JVP (6 cm) with prominent v wave; ascites and tender hepatomegaly on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: RV heave with a loud P2 and elevated JVP is the cardinal examination signature of right ventricular pressure overload and failure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Right ventricular hypertrophy/strain and elevated pulmonary pressures on echocardiogram

investigation_details.expected_result: Right ventricular hypertrophy/dilatation with signs of pressure overload and elevated estimated pulmonary artery pressure
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cor pulmonale remains unconfirmed and pulmonary pressures/RV function uncharacterised.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Echocardiographic RV hypertrophy/strain with pulmonary hypertension confirms cor pulmonale.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Decompensated cor pulmonale secondary to severe COPD
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Worsening exertional dyspnoea with resting hypoxaemia, massive bilateral oedema, and new ascites/congestive hepatomegaly on a background of severe COPD together establish decompensated right-heart failure requiring urgent controlled oxygen and diuresis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Arterial blood gas / controlled pulse oximetry
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assesses hypoxaemia and, crucially, CO2 retention before and during oxygen therapy in COPD.
Confirms RV hypertrophy/strain and estimates pulmonary artery pressure to establish cor pulmonale.
Required before and during diuresis to give it safely and detect electrolyte disturbance or renal impairment.
ECG for right-heart strain/P pulmonale and arrhythmia; chest X-ray for cardiomegaly, pulmonary changes and to exclude alternative causes.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Advising uncontrolled high-flow oxygen at home without CO2 monitoring
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Uncontrolled oxygen in COPD risks CO2 narcosis; oxygen must be titrated to target saturations with monitoring.
